Title: Natural allelic variation in the CBF2 transcription factor is a pivotal factor controlling cold resistance in potato

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in potato; RT-qPCR; sugar metabolite profiling by LC-MS; frost phenotype; electrolyte leakage assay; Fv/Fm measurement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases raffinose accumulation and enhances freezing/cold tolerance; also elevates glucose and sucrose in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScGolS3 overexpression in cold-sensitive potato E3 significantly increased GolS3 transcript abundance. 2. Transgenic lines accumulated more raffinose, sucrose, and glucose in leaves than WT. 3. Overexpression lines showed less frost injury, lower electrolyte leakage, and higher Fv/Fm, demonstrating improved cold resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Compatible solute associated with stress tolerance and cold/freezing resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes galactinol biosynthesis, supplying the galactosyl donor required for raffinose formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme involved in raffinose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cold induced; higher expression in S. commersonii than in E3 at 4°C during early cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed under normal temperature and after cold acclimation in transgenic E3 leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold acclimation at 4°C for 7 d; frost treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotypic difference from WT under normal conditions reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase raffinose-associated freezing tolerance in potato, particularly for engineering cold-sensitive cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ScGolS3 and StGolS3 proteins have extremely high amino acid similarity and the same domain structure, suggesting similar enzyme function; differential regulation rather than enzyme divergence explains phenotypes

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; promoter activation assay; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its expression is strongly activated by ScCBF2 but only weakly/transiently by StCBF2, leading to higher raffinose under cold acclimation when ScCBF2 is present
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GolS3 was the only GolS family member significantly induced by ScCBF2. 2. ScCBF2 directly bound GolS3 promoter motifs and activated transcription more strongly than StCBF2. 3. Elevated GolS3 expression correlated with increased raffinose accumulation and stronger cold tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in raffinose biosynthesis via galactinol production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme involved in raffinose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in ScCBF2-overexpression lines than in StCBF2-overexpression lines, especially after cold acclimation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can serve as a downstream engineering module for raising raffinose and cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GolS1 and GolS2 were not notably changed in transgenic lines; GolS3 is the specific responsive family member

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family; CBF/DREB1 transcription factor; activation domain; nuclear localization signal; AP2 domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allelic comparison across genotypes; overexpression in potato; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Dual-LUC; GUS transient assay; EMSA; molecular docking; sugar metabolite profiling; frost phenotype; electrolyte leakage; Fv/Fm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enhances constitutive and acclimated freezing tolerance in potato; upregulates GolS3 and increases raffinose accumulation; also induces antioxidant/cold-response genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural site A variation in ScCBF2 is associated with strong cold tolerance among potato germplasm. 2. Overexpression of ScCBF2 in E3 confers stronger freezing tolerance than StCBF2 without reducing tuber yield, though causing dwarfing. 3. ScCBF2 directly binds the GolS3 promoter more strongly than StCBF2, markedly activates GolS3 transcription, and increases raffinose accumulation after cold acclimation. 4. ScCBF2 also upregulates GSTU7, GSTU8, and ZAT10, supporting ROS detoxification and cold resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-protective compatible sugar contributing to freezing/cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GolS3; GSTU7; GSTU8; ZAT10; COR like; LEA27
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GolS3 strongly; upregulates GSTU7, GSTU8, ZAT10, COR-like and LEA27
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via binding CRT/DRE motifs in promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator; stronger than StCBF2 because site A enhances promoter binding and GolS3 activation, making it pivotal for raffinose-linked cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold acclimation at 4°C for 7 d; frost treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cold induced; expression pattern similar to StCBF2 during cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively overexpressed in transgenic E3 under normal and cold-acclimated conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes mild dwarfing/vegetative growth retardation but no significant change in tuber weight, tuber number, or tuber morphology
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising allele for potato breeding or engineering to improve freezing tolerance without yield penalty; site A is a valuable editing target
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Site A serine-proline type is associated with cold tolerance; site A enhances binding affinity to GolS3 promoter motifs B1/B2 and transcriptional activation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in potato; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Dual-LUC; GUS transient assay; EMSA; molecular docking; frost phenotype; electrolyte leakage; Fv/Fm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cold tolerance, but more weakly than ScCBF2; has limited effect on GolS3 expression and does not elevate raffinose substantially after cold acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StCBF2 overexpression improves freezing tolerance compared with WT but less than ScCBF2. 2. It upregulates GSTU7, GSTU8, and ZAT10, supporting ROS scavenging-based cold resistance. 3. It binds the GolS3 promoter more weakly and has limited transcriptional activation, resulting in little change in raffinose accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-associated sugar linked to freezing tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cold-response transcription and antioxidant pathways; weak control of raffinose biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GSTU7; GSTU8; ZAT10; COR like; LEA27; weakly GolS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates GSTU7, GSTU8, ZAT10 and related cold genes; weak/transient activation of GolS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via promoter binding, but with weaker activation of GolS3 than ScCBF2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally positive but not decisive for raffinose accumulation because weak GolS3 activation limits downstream metabolite increase
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold acclimation at 4°C for 7 d; frost treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively overexpressed in E3 transgenic lines under normal and cold-acclimated conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes mild dwarfing but no significant effect on tuber yield traits reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful but inferior allele compared with ScCBF2; improving site A may enhance performance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Structural variation at site A underlies weaker GolS3 promoter binding and lower transcriptional activation than ScCBF2
